claim: Agent harness engineering—not model selection—is the primary bottleneck in agent reliability
actionable: When your agent fails, audit the harness (tools, context window management, memory patterns) before upgrading the model
source_title: Harness Engineering: The Skill That Will Define 2026 for Solo Devs

claim: Vercel reduced agent accuracy from 80% to 100% by removing 80% of specialized tools and providing generic bash/file access
actionable: Simplify agent tool suites: prefer generic CLI tools (bash, file I/O) over domain-specific abstractions to reduce context noise
source_title: Harness Engineering: The Skill That Will Define 2026 for Solo Devs

claim: Tool calls compound exponentially in long agent sessions; use external files for state management to reduce context
actionable: Have agents maintain progress files (to-do lists, state logs) on disk and read them at session start instead of relying on message history
source_title: Harness Engineering: The Skill That Will Define 2026 for Solo Devs

claim: As models improve, agent harnesses should get simpler, not more complex—overengineering harnesses is counterproductive
actionable: Resist adding custom tooling and routing logic; instead simplify harnesses and rely on model improvements as LLMs scale
source_title: Harness Engineering: The Skill That Will Define 2026 for Solo Devs

claim: MCP and skills provide clean extension points for agents without hardcoding integrations
actionable: Use MCP and skills for extensibility instead of custom tool APIs baked into the agent harness
source_title: Harness Engineering: The Skill That Will Define 2026 for Solo Devs
